ucs.title: [LLM] MoustachedBouncer fake Windows/Java Update dropped via ISP HTTP content-injection
ucs.description: Hunts MoustachedBouncer's signature initial-access chain: Belarusian ISP-level adversary-in-the-middle (T1659) redirects an embassy host's plain-HTTP traffic to a lookalike 'Windows Update' page, which delivers the Disco/NightClub installer that masquerades as a Microsoft or Java updater. Detection fires on file drops named like update binaries whose FileOriginUrl is plain HTTP from a non-Microsoft/non-Oracle host, followed by execution within an hour.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstSeen max(_time) as lastSeen from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action=created (Filesystem.file_name="*update*" OR Filesystem.file_name="*wuauclt*" OR Filesystem.file_name="*WindowsUpdate*" OR Filesystem.file_name="*MicrosoftUpdate*" OR Filesystem.file_name="*JavaUpdate*" OR Filesystem.file_name="*JavaSetup*") (Filesystem.file_name="*.exe" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.msi" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.dll") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_hash Filesystem.process_guid Filesystem.file_origin_url
| `drop_dm_object_name("Filesystem")`
| where like(file_origin_url, "http://%") AND NOT match(file_origin_url, "(?i)(microsoft\.com|windowsupdate\.com|update\.microsoft|oracle\.com|java\.com|sun\.com|akamai)")
| join type=inner dest file_hash [
    | tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as execTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.dest Processes.process_hash
    | rename Processes.dest as dest Processes.process_hash as file_hash
  ]
| where execTime >= firstSeen AND execTime <= firstSeen + 3600
| table firstSeen execTime dest user file_name file_hash file_origin_url cmdline parent
ucs.kql:
// MoustachedBouncer (G1019) — ISP-injected fake Windows Update lure
let _ms_update_hosts = dynamic(["microsoft.com","windowsupdate.com","update.microsoft","windows.com","oracle.com","java.com","sun.com","akamaiedge.net","akamaitechnologies.com"]);
let Drops =
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where FileName has_any ("update","wuauclt","WindowsUpdate","MicrosoftUpdate","JavaUpdate","JavaSetup","jusched")
    | where FileName endswith ".exe" or FileName endswith ".msi" or FileName endswith ".dll"
    | where isnotempty(FileOriginUrl)
    | where FileOriginUrl startswith "http://"          // ISP injection cannot rewrite TLS — only plaintext HTTP
    | extend OriginHost = tolower(tostring(parse_url(FileOriginUrl).Host))
    | where not(OriginHost has_any (_ms_update_hosts))
    | project DropTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, DroppedFile = FileName,
              FolderPath, SHA256, FileOriginUrl, FileOriginIP, OriginHost,
              InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessAccountName;
Drops
| join kind=inner (
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | project ExecTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, ExecFile = FileName, SHA256,
              ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName1 = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              AccountName
  ) on DeviceId, SHA256
| where ExecTime between (DropTime .. DropTime + 1h)
| project DropTime, ExecTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', ExecTime, DropTime),
          DeviceName, AccountName, DroppedFile, OriginHost, FileOriginUrl,
          FileOriginIP, SHA256, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName1
| order by DropTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// MoustachedBouncer (G1019) — fake update download over HTTP from non-Microsoft host
let _ms_update_hosts = dynamic(["microsoft.com","windowsupdate.com","update.microsoft","windows.com","oracle.com","java.com","sun.com","akamaiedge.net","akamaitechnologies.com"]);
ImWebSession
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where HttpMethod =~ "GET"
| where Url startswith "http://"                                  // plaintext only — TLS would defeat ISP injection
| where Url has_any ("update", "wuauclt", "WindowsUpdate", "MicrosoftUpdate", "JavaUpdate", "JavaSetup")
| where Url endswith ".exe" or Url endswith ".msi" or Url endswith ".dll" or Url endswith ".cab"
| extend Host = tolower(tostring(coalesce(DstHostname, HttpHostName)))
| where not(Host has_any (_ms_update_hosts))
| project DownloadTime = TimeGenerated, SrcIpAddr, SrcHostname, SrcUsername,
          Url, Host, DstIpAddr, FileName, HttpRequestHeaderUserAgent
| join kind=inner (
    ImProcessCreate
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
    | where TargetProcessName has_any ("update","wuauclt","WindowsUpdate","MicrosoftUpdate","JavaUpdate","JavaSetup")
    | project ExecTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
              TargetProcessName, TargetProcessCommandLine, ParentProcessName, SHA256
  ) on $left.SrcHostname == $right.DvcHostname
| where ExecTime between (DownloadTime .. DownloadTime + 1h)
| project DownloadTime, ExecTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', ExecTime, DownloadTime),
          DvcHostname, ActorUsername, Host, Url, TargetProcessName,
          TargetProcessCommandLine, ParentProcessName, SHA256
| order by DownloadTime desc
ucs.rationale: ESET's August 2023 reporting on MoustachedBouncer documents that the group has, since 2014, abused lawful-intercept capability at Beltelecom / Unitary Enterprise A1 to inject HTTP responses into traffic from foreign embassies in Belarus, redirecting them to a fake 'Windows Update Center' that delivers Disco or NightClub. The combination 'plain-HTTP download of an update-named binary from a non-Mic

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `WindowsUpdate` is not a canonical column of ImWebSession.
`MicrosoftUpdate` is not a canonical column of ImWebSession.
`JavaUpdate` is not a canonical column of ImWebSession.
`JavaSetup` is not a canonical column of ImWebSession.
`ActorUsername` is not a column of ImWebSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImAuthentication, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
`TargetProcessName` is not a column of ImWebSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate.
`TargetProcessCommandLine` is not a column of ImWebSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate.
`ParentProcessName` is not a column of ImWebSession; it does exist on SecurityEvent, ImProcessCreate.
`SHA256` is not a column of ImWebSession; it does exist on DeviceProcessEvents, DeviceFileEvents, DeviceImageLoadEvents, DeviceEvents, EmailAttachmentInfo, AlertEvidence, ImProcessCreate.
`DownloadTime` is not a canonical column of ImWebSession.
`Host` is not a canonical column of ImWebSession.

ucs.title: [LLM] MoustachedBouncer Disco SMB-share staging & internal proxy (T1074.002 + T1090)
ucs.description: Hunts the Disco backdoor's signature C2 architecture — because target embassy networks are often segmented from the internet, MoustachedBouncer stages payloads, plugins and exfil on an internal SMB share that an internet-facing 'proxy' host then ferries out. Detection looks for scripting engines (cscript/wscript/powershell) or rundll32 writing executable / script content to remote SMB paths, paired with a different host reading the same files shortly after.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstWrite max(_time) as lastWrite values(Filesystem.process_name) as writer_proc values(Filesystem.user) as writer_user from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action=created (Filesystem.process_name IN ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe")) (Filesystem.file_path="\\\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\C$\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\ADMIN$\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\IPC$\\*") (Filesystem.file_name="*.js" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.vbs" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.ps1" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.exe" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.bin" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.dat") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_hash
| `drop_dm_object_name("Filesystem")`
| rename dest as writer_host
| join type=inner file_hash [
    | tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as readTime values(Filesystem.dest) as reader_host from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action IN ("read","opened","modified") by Filesystem.file_hash Filesystem.dest
    | `drop_dm_object_name("Filesystem")`
  ]
| where writer_host!=reader_host AND readTime >= firstWrite AND readTime <= firstWrite + 3600
| table firstWrite readTime writer_host reader_host writer_proc writer_user file_name file_path file_hash
ucs.kql:
// MoustachedBouncer (G1019) — Disco SMB staging: script engine writes payload to remote share, then a peer host opens it
let _scripting = dynamic(["wscript.exe","cscript.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe"]);
let _payload_ext = dynamic([".js",".vbs",".ps1",".dll",".exe",".bin",".dat",".tmp"]);
let RemoteWrites =
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified")
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_scripting)
    | where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
    // Remote SMB target — folder path starts with UNC, or DeviceFileEvents reports SMB protocol
    | where (FolderPath startswith @"\\") or (RequestProtocol in~ ("SMB","SMB2"))
    | where FolderPath has_any (@"\C$\", @"\ADMIN$\", @"\IPC$\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\") or FolderPath startswith @"\\"
    | where FileName endswith_any (_payload_ext)
    | project WriteTime = Timestamp, WriterHost = DeviceName, WriterDeviceId = DeviceId,
              WriterProc = InitiatingProcessFileName, WriterCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              WriterUser = InitiatingProcessAccountName, ShareName, FolderPath, FileName,
              SHA256, RequestSourceIP;
let RemoteReads =
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
    | project ReadTime = Timestamp, ReaderHost = DeviceName, ReaderDeviceId = DeviceId,
              ReaderProc = InitiatingProcessFileName, SHA256, FileName;
RemoteWrites
| join kind=inner RemoteReads on SHA256
| where ReaderDeviceId != WriterDeviceId
| where ReadTime between (WriteTime .. WriteTime + 1h)
| summarize Writes = count(),
            ReaderHosts = make_set(ReaderHost, 10),
            ReaderProcs = make_set(ReaderProc, 10),
            FirstWrite = min(WriteTime),
            FirstRead = min(ReadTime)
            by WriterHost, WriterUser, WriterProc, WriterCmd, FolderPath, FileName, SHA256
| where array_length(ReaderHosts) >= 1                       // proxy host pulled the staged file
| order by FirstWrite desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// MoustachedBouncer (G1019) — SMB staging via ASIM (script engine writes UNC payload, peer host reads it)
let _scripting = dynamic(["wscript.exe","cscript.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe"]);
let _payload_ext = dynamic([".js",".vbs",".ps1",".dll",".exe",".bin",".dat",".tmp"]);
let RemoteWrites =
    ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileModified")
    | where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
    | where ActingProcessName has_any (_scripting)
    | where TargetFilePath startswith @"\\"                  // UNC = remote SMB write
    | where TargetFilePath has_any (@"\C$\", @"\ADMIN$\", @"\IPC$\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\") or TargetFilePath startswith @"\\"
    | where TargetFileExtension in~ (_payload_ext) or TargetFileName endswith_any (_payload_ext)
    | project WriteTime = TimeGenerated, WriterHost = DvcHostname, WriterUser = ActorUsername,
              WriterProc = ActingProcessName, WriterCmd = ActingProcessCommandLine,
              TargetFilePath, TargetFileName, TargetFileSHA256;
let RemoteReads =
    ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileAccessed","FileRenamed")
    | project ReadTime = TimeGenerated, ReaderHost = DvcHostname, ReaderProc = ActingProcessName, TargetFileSHA256;
RemoteWrites
| join kind=inner RemoteReads on TargetFileSHA256
| where ReaderHost != WriterHost
| where ReadTime between (WriteTime .. WriteTime + 1h)
| summarize Writes = count(),
            ReaderHosts = make_set(ReaderHost, 10),
            ReaderProcs = make_set(ReaderProc, 10),
            FirstWrite = min(WriteTime),
            FirstRead = min(ReadTime)
            by WriterHost, WriterUser, WriterProc, WriterCmd, TargetFilePath, TargetFileName, TargetFileSHA256
| where array_length(ReaderHosts) >= 1
| order by FirstWrite desc
ucs.rationale: ESET's MoustachedBouncer technical write-up details that the Disco implant family is built specifically to exploit segmented embassy networks: rather than calling out to the internet directly from every infected workstation, Disco copies plugins, output and tasking through SMB shares to a single 'proxy' host that has the only egress path — a direct expression of the actor's MITRE-mapped T1074.002
